tokenAuth.description: Send the header ``Authorization: Token <key>`` where ``<key>`` is a staff-owned token from the Studio tokens page. New and rotated token values are shown once in Studio and cannot be retrieved later. The literal scheme name is ``Token``, not ``Bearer`` (Swagger UI's authorize dialog renders this as ``bearer`` but the wire format we accept is ``Token``).

info.description:
Operator API for AI Shipping Labs. All endpoints accept JSON in and return JSON out. Authentication is via the ``Authorization: Token <key>`` header where ``<key>`` is a token owned by a staff user. Studio shows new or rotated operator token values once; existing plaintext tokens cannot be retrieved later.

The spec endpoint ``/api/openapi.json`` itself accepts the same ``Authorization: Token <key>`` header, so OpenAPI tooling (Postman ``Import -> Link``, ``openapi-generator``, Swagger UI) can pull the spec from the same base URL it then calls. Example:

```
curl -H "Authorization: Token $API_TOKEN" https://aishippinglabs.com/api/openapi.json > spec.json
```
info.title: AI Shipping Labs Operator API

200.description: Preview URL for the synced article

get.description: Returns every ``EmailCampaign`` row ordered by ``-created_at``. Optional filters narrow the result.

schema.description: Filter to campaigns with the given status.

campaigns.subject: Week 3 of May 2026 sprint

post.description: Creates an ``EmailCampaign`` row in ``draft`` status. Sending stays a deliberate Studio action -- this API intentionally has no ``/send`` route. Read-only fields (``id``, ``status``, ``sent_at``, ``sent_count``, ``created_at``) in the body are rejected with 422 ``read_only_field``; ``status`` in the body is silently overwritten to ``draft`` so detail round-trips keep working.

target_event.description: Event id to target registrants, or null.

example.body: Hello {{ first_name }} — here's what's up this week...
example.subject: Week 3 of May 2026 sprint

example.subject: Week 3 of May 2026 sprint

get.description: Returns campaign recipient visibility for staff automation. Draft campaigns return ``mode=preview`` from the same ``EmailCampaign.get_eligible_recipients()`` audience logic used by Studio. Sending/sent campaigns return ``mode=sent`` with actual ``EmailLog`` rows, including engagement and bounce/complaint disposition.

patch.description: Partial update. Changing ``position`` re-packs sibling positions inside the same week (atomically).

post.description: Atomic cross-week move + reorder. Locks both affected weeks in id order (deadlock-safe) inside one ``transaction.atomic``. Returns a reconciliation envelope so an optimistic client does not need a follow-up GET. Idempotent when source week and position are unchanged.

get.description: Default response is JSON. ``?format=csv`` switches to ``text/csv`` with an ``aishippinglabs-contacts-<utc-timestamp>.csv`` attachment header. Output is ordered by ``id`` so repeat calls are deterministic.

post.description: Per-row tags are MERGED into the user's existing tags (idempotent append, not replace). Per-row ``tier`` is accepted only when live Stripe confirms the active subscription maps to the same tier; otherwise the row is warned. Tier overrides are intentionally not available through this endpoint.

400.description: Malformed body or unknown default tier.

post.description: Replaces (NOT merges) the user's tags with the supplied normalized list. An empty list clears tags.

400.description: Malformed body or missing tags list.

post.description: Upsert on ``(user, course)``. Existing rows update only ``pdf_url`` and ``submission_id``; ``issued_at`` is immutable (it's the historical issue date and ``auto_now_add``).

get.description: Active enrollments by default; ``?include_unenrolled=1`` includes soft-deleted rows. Staff sees all rows; non-staff sees only their own.

post.description: Accepts either ``{"user_email": "..."}`` (single) or ``{"user_emails": [...]}`` (bulk). Tier mismatches are flagged as ``under_tier`` (warning) but the enrollment is still created -- staff are explicitly choosing to enroll the user.

422.description: Missing user_email / user_emails or bad type.

delete.description: Staff-only. Idempotent: returns 204 whether or not a row was changed. Soft-deletes the active enrollment by setting ``unenrolled_at``; the historical row is preserved so a follow-up POST can create a new active row. There is no GET / PATCH on this URL.
delete.summary: Unenroll a user (soft-delete)

get.description: Returns the complete per-user CRM aggregate (core user state, ``crm_record``, ``notes``, nested ``plans``, sprint and course enrollments, and ``onboarding_responses``) in a single response, reusing the per-resource serializers so the shapes are identical. Staff-token only. Internal-visibility notes ARE included (this is the staff CRM surface). Defaults to ``scope=crm`` (only members carrying CRM signal); pass ``scope=all`` for the full user table. Ordered by ``User.id``; ``count`` is the page size, ``total`` the full match before slicing. Operator automation that needs one user should pass ``email=`` for an exact, case-insensitive lookup; this constrains the queryset before aggregate serialization instead of scanning a broad export. When ``email`` and ``q`` are both supplied, ``email`` wins.

schema.description: ``crm`` (default) returns only users with CRM signal (a CRMRecord, a note, a plan, an onboarding response, or a non-system tag). ``all`` returns every user. Unknown value -> 422.
Default 200; clamped to the configurable ``CRM_EXPORT_MAX_LIMIT`` ceiling.
ISO-8601 datetime; only users with ``date_joined >= since``.
Substring search across email / name / Stripe id / Slack id / tags (same semantics as ``GET /api/users``). Ignored when ``email`` is supplied.

Exact case-insensitive user email lookup for fast targeted CRM automation. Surrounding whitespace is trimmed. Unknown users return 404 ``user_not_found``. Use this instead of broad ``scope=all`` exports when fetching one user.

404.description: ``email`` was supplied but no exact user exists.

delete.summary: Delete a deliverable

get.description: Returns the most recent per-phase cold-start timings for both the web and worker container tiers, each with its build ``tag``, ``recorded_at``, ``role``, and a ``phases`` map, plus a top-level ``generated_at``. Staff-token only, read-only. A tier with no captured boot yet is ``null``; the response is still a clean 200 (never 404/500).

200.description: The latest web and worker boot timings.

get.summary: Read the latest per-phase container boot timings

get.description: Returns the production-data gate counts the blocked cleanup issues (#1016 / #1018 / #1017) wait on, plus a ``generated_at`` timestamp. Staff-token only, read-only. Integer counts only -- no PII, no row ids, no titles. Each gate proceeds only when its count is ``0``.

delete.description: DELETE returns 405 with a structured error -- deletion is not exposed through the API. Hide the series with ``PATCH is_active=false``.

example.error: Event series deletion is not available through the API. Hide the series with PATCH is_active=false, or use Studio to delete it.

required_level.description: Canonical access level (0/10/20/30) the series' occurrences inherit and are validated against (issue #958). Changing it never rewrites existing occurrences.

example.error: Event series deletion is not available through the API. Hide the series with PATCH is_active=false, or use Studio to delete it.

get.description: Returns the series plus an inlined ``occurrences`` array using the same shape as the events API.

patch.description: Partial update. Setting ``is_active=false`` hides the series without touching its occurrences.

required_level.description: Canonical access level (0/10/20/30). Changing it does NOT rewrite existing occurrences; future occurrence writes are validated against the new value (issue #958).

put.description:
Idempotent schedule-replace. Unlike the additive ``POST .../occurrences/bulk`` (which only adds), this declares the FULL desired set in one atomic transaction:
- desired dates with no matching occurrence are created (same per-row defaults as the bulk creator; new drafts carry ``published=False`` per the #878 visibility contract),
- desired dates matching an existing non-cancelled occurrence are kept untouched (dedup by minute-rounded ``start_datetime``),
- a desired date matching a CANCELLED occurrence reactivates that row to ``status='upcoming'`` instead of creating a duplicate,
- every other currently non-cancelled occurrence is CANCELLED (``status='cancelled'`` — never hard-deleted, per the no-deletes-via-API policy #864).

Re-submitting the same set is a no-op. In-batch duplicate desired dates return 422 ``duplicate_in_batch``. Any per-row validation failure rolls the whole reconcile back and reports the failing index.

required_level.description: Optional. Defaults to the series' required_level. For created/updated rows it MUST equal the series level or the reconcile returns 422 level_mismatch and rolls back. Reactivating a cancelled occurrence does NOT change its stored level (issue #958).

example.error: Occurrence access level does not match the series. Adjust the level to match the series, or make the change in Studio.

422.description: Validation error, in-batch duplicate start_datetime, or a created/updated row whose required_level differs from the series level (level_mismatch, issue #958 — rolls back).

put.summary: Declare the exact desired occurrence set

post.description: Atomic create of N events linked to ``series_id``. Any per-row validation failure rolls the whole batch back; the response includes the failing index. Dedup key is ``(event_series_id, start_datetime)`` rounded to the minute -- in-batch duplicates return 422 ``duplicate_in_batch``, and rows matching existing series events are silently counted as ``skipped_existing`` for idempotent retries.

required_level.description: Optional. Defaults to (inherits) the series' required_level. If supplied it MUST equal the series level — a mismatch returns 422 level_mismatch and rolls back the batch (issue #958).

example.error: Occurrence access level does not match the series. Adjust the level to match the series, or make the change in Studio.

422.description: Validation error, in-batch duplicate start_datetime, or a row whose required_level differs from the series level (level_mismatch, issue #958 — rolls the whole batch back).

delete.description: Cancel an occurrence with ``PATCH status=cancelled`` instead. DELETE returns a structured 405.

example.error: Occurrence deletion is not available through the API. Cancel with PATCH status="cancelled", or use Studio to delete it.

delete.summary: DELETE is not available on this route

404.description: Event series or occurrence not found.

patch.description: Cancel with ``{"status": "cancelled"}``. The API does NOT auto-send attendee notifications in v1.

required_level.description: Setting this to a value differing from the series' required_level is rejected with 422 level_mismatch (no index); the stored level is left unchanged (issue #958).

example.error: Occurrence access level does not match the series. Adjust the level to match the series, or make the change in Studio.

422.description: Validation error, attempt to write a read-only field, or a required_level differing from the series level (level_mismatch, issue #958).

post.description:
Programmatic equivalent of the Studio "Create Zoom meetings for all events" button (#859). Enqueues the same idempotent background job that creates a Zoom meeting for every eligible occurrence -- future (``is_upcoming``), ``platform='zoom'``, and no existing ``zoom_meeting_id``. Past / cancelled / draft and ``custom``-platform occurrences are skipped. The request returns immediately; the N Zoom API round-trips run on the worker.

Pass ``{"dry_run": true}`` to get the eligibility count WITHOUT enqueuing or calling Zoom. Unknown top-level keys are ignored; a body that is present but not a JSON object returns 422.

Idempotent: occurrences that already carry a ``zoom_meeting_id`` are never recreated, so re-POSTing is safe and returns ``noop`` once nothing is left to create. Two rapid POSTs may enqueue two jobs, but the second job creates nothing for already-handled occurrences; there is no locking in v1.

No DELETE / remove counterpart is exposed (no-deletes-via-API policy) -- recreating or removing meetings would orphan live join links already mailed to attendees. Read the run result from ``zoom_meetings_last_run`` on ``GET /api/event-series/<id>``.

dry_run.description: Preview the eligible count without enqueuing or calling Zoom.

200.description: Either a dry-run preview, or a noop when nothing is eligible.

example.error: Body must be a JSON object

422.description: Body present but not a JSON object.

post.summary: Create Zoom meetings for every eligible occurrence

post.description: Studio/API-origin events can be created through this endpoint. GitHub-origin events are read-only here.

create_zoom.description: Write-only action trigger. When true and platform is 'zoom', provisions a real Zoom meeting and populates zoom_join_url / zoom_meeting_id. Idempotent; never stored or returned. On Zoom failure the event still persists and the body carries a non-fatal zoom_error key.

generate_banner.description: Write-only action trigger. Defaults to true on create: the API auto-renders the 1200x630 social/banner image in the background. Set false to skip. Never stored or returned. When enqueued the response carries banner_task_id (poll /api/worker/tasks/<id>); when the generator is unconfigured the event still persists and the body carries a non-fatal banner_error key.

host_email.description: Optional. Email address of a platform user who should be auto-registered as the event host attendee. Blank or non-resolvable emails do not create a host registration.

host_ids.description: Optional. Ordered event host ids. Empty array clears all hosts.

materials.description: Ordered recording materials. Each item needs a title and URL; type is optional. Empty array clears materials.

recording_url.description: Optional primary recording URL. Empty string clears the field.

timestamps.description: Recording chapters. Empty array clears chapters on PATCH; omitted PATCH values preserve existing chapters.

items.description: Provide either time_seconds or time, and either label or title. Stored responses always return canonical time_seconds/label rows.

time_seconds.description: Canonical seconds value. Integer-like strings are accepted and normalized to integers.

patch.description: Synced GitHub events are read-only through this API and return 409 ``synced_event_read_only``.

create_zoom.description: Write-only action trigger. When true and the event's platform is 'zoom' with no existing meeting, provisions a Zoom meeting and populates zoom_join_url / zoom_meeting_id. Idempotent; never stored or returned. On Zoom failure the event still persists and the body carries a non-fatal zoom_error key.

generate_banner.description: Write-only action trigger. Explicit-only on PATCH (default off): only an explicit true re-enqueues a forced banner render after the update is saved, so an unrelated edit never silently re-renders. Never stored or returned.

host_email.description: Optional. Email address of a platform user who should be auto-registered as the event host attendee. Adding a resolvable host to a published upcoming event sends the normal registration confirmation. Empty string clears the field.

host_ids.description: Optional. Ordered event host ids. Empty array clears all hosts.

materials.description: Ordered recording materials. Each item needs a title and URL; type is optional. Empty array clears materials.

recording_url.description: Optional primary recording URL. Empty string clears the field.

timestamps.description: Recording chapters. Empty array clears chapters on PATCH; omitted PATCH values preserve existing chapters.

items.description: Provide either time_seconds or time, and either label or title. Stored responses always return canonical time_seconds/label rows.

time_seconds.description: Canonical seconds value. Integer-like strings are accepted and normalized to integers.

post.description: Sends the event-level workshop-ready transactional email to active event registrants plus host recipients, creates in-app notifications for platform users, and skips recipients already sent via the per-recipient idempotency key. The event must have a linked published workshop.

example.error: A linked published workshop is required.

422.description: The event has no linked published workshop.

post.description: Force-enqueues a banner-generator render for an existing event and returns the django-q task id. The render runs as a background task; the resolved banner_url stays empty until the worker finishes, so poll GET /api/worker/tasks/<task_id> (or re-GET the event for banner_url). Regeneration is ALLOWED for synced/GitHub-origin events: the PATCH read-only rule protects content fields owned by the source repo, but the auto-banner is derived presentation state the platform owns regardless of origin (the sync pipeline itself enqueues renders for synced records), so there is no 409 here. Returns 422 banner_generator_not_configured (no enqueue) when the generator is not configured.

example.error: Banner generator is not configured. Add the function URL and bearer token under Studio Settings first.

The actual stored or env value is NEVER included.

post.description: All-or-nothing batch: any unknown key fails the entire batch with 400 ``invalid_key``. Empty-string on a non-boolean key clears the DB override. Response never echoes key names or values.

example.error: One or more keys are not in the integration registry

get.description: Newest-first list of ``SlackChannelIngest`` runs so an operator can verify a backfill worked and read its tallies WITHOUT a Studio login. Each run carries its ``status`` (running/success/error), ``started_at`` / ``finished_at``, the Slack ts window pulled (``oldest_ts`` / ``latest_ts``), and the count fields: ``messages_seen``, ``threads_persisted``, ``replies_added``, ``members_matched`` (plus ``error`` text for failed runs). Read-only — it never echoes Slack message content, only the per-run counts/metadata the model already stores. Token-gated (staff tokens only).
get.summary: List recent #plan-sprints ingest runs with counts

post.description: Kicks off the same capture + parse + auto-apply path the daily schedule runs. With no body it uses the forward watermark (or the 7-day first-run default). Pass ``since`` (``YYYY-MM-DD``, UTC midnight) to retroactively read OLDER history. ``dry_run=true`` runs the full path then rolls it back so nothing is persisted. The work runs on the background worker; the response carries the task id. The ingest is idempotent, so a duplicate trigger never double-applies progress.

dry_run.description: Run and roll back without persisting.

since.description: YYYY-MM-DD; read history from this date.

409.description: Ingestion is not available: Slack is disabled or the #plan-sprints channel is not configured.

get.description: Lists ``Persona`` rows -- internal archetypes, never shown to members (staff-token only). ``default_questionnaire`` is the linked questionnaire ``slug`` or ``null``. Ordered by the model default (``order, name``).

Lists ``Questionnaire`` rows with nested ordered base ``Question`` rows and their ordered ``QuestionOption`` rows. Reads the BASE template -- this describes the live survey shape, not any member's snapshot. Questionnaires are newest-first; questions and options follow their model ordering. No pagination (the set is small and staff-authored).

schema.description: Exact match on ``purpose`` (one of ``onboarding`` / ``feedback`` / ``general``). Unknown value -> 422.

get.description: Lists onboarding responses (the shared response object per item) for batch plan generation. Defaults to ``status=submitted`` (only finished responses); pass ``status=draft`` or ``status=all`` to widen. ``since`` is an inclusive lower bound on ``submitted_at`` when filtering submitted, else on ``created_at``. ``persona`` filters by resolved persona ``slug`` (or the literal ``none`` for responses with no resolved persona). Each item includes an additive ``crm_record`` relationship when a CRM record exists. ``count`` is the TOTAL matching set BEFORE slicing.

schema.description: ISO-8601 datetime; inclusive lower bound on ``submitted_at`` (submitted) or ``created_at``.
Persona ``slug`` to filter by resolved persona, or ``none`` for responses with no resolved persona. Unknown slug -> 422.

questions.prompt: On a scale of 1-5, how confident are you with ML?

get.description: Returns the member's single onboarding ``Response`` (draft OR submitted -- ``status`` / ``submitted_at`` tell which) as the shared response object: ``email``, ``questionnaire_slug``, ``status``, ``submitted_at``, resolved ``persona`` (or ``null``), additive ``crm_record`` relationship (or ``null``), and an ordered flat ``questions`` Q&A list read from the SNAPSHOT (``ResponseQuestion`` / ``Answer`` / ``ResponseQuestionOption``), never the base ``Question``. Email lookup is case-insensitive.

questions.prompt: On a scale of 1-5, how confident are you with ML?

post.description: Staff-only. Runs the same shared service as the Studio "Draft next sprint plan" button: carry-over of unfinished tasks always runs first; an LLM draft of the next-sprint narrative is produced and stored aside ONLY when the LLM service is enabled. The draft is never written into the plan's live fields. With the LLM off this is a 200 with ``draft: null`` (NOT an error) and carry-over still ran.

404.description: Plan not found or not visible.

get.summary: List interview notes for a plan

post.description: Staff-token-only. Atomically moves unfinished Checkpoint, Deliverable, and NextStep rows from the source plan into the same member's later target sprint. Completed items and all non-work-item plan content remain on the source plan. If the target plan does not exist, it is created through the sprint enrollment helper.

400.description: Invalid JSON, non-object body, or missing target_sprint_slug.

404.description: Unknown source plan or target sprint.

example.error: Target sprint must be later than the source sprint

422.description: Cancelled target, same/earlier target, source plan with zero unfinished items, or target plan without weeks.

404.description: Plan not found or not visible.
Plan not found or not visible.

example.error: Week number already exists for this plan

409.description: A week with the same week_number already exists for this plan.
A redirect with the same source_path already exists.

422.description: Validation error: source_path == target_path (loop), unknown redirect_type, missing field.

post.description: Atomic upsert by ``source_path``. Any row failure rolls every row back. ``action`` is ``created`` / ``updated`` / ``skipped`` (where ``skipped`` means the row was identical to the existing record).

delete.description: Redirect deletion is not available through the API (issue #864); use Studio to delete, or deactivate with ``PATCH is_active=false``. DELETE returns a structured 405.

example.error: Redirect deletion is not available through the API. Go to Studio to delete this redirect manually, or deactivate it with PATCH is_active=false.

delete.summary: DELETE is not available on this route

409.description: Source path conflict with another redirect.

422.description: Validation error or loop check failed.

delete.description: Re-packs sibling positions inside the same plan.
delete.summary: Delete a resource

get.description: Newest-first list of ``SesEvent`` rows across every recipient, including events whose ``user`` FK is ``None`` (addresses with no ``User`` row). Lets an operator reconcile a campaign's bounces by window, campaign (``email_log``), recipient substring, or event type. ``raw_payload`` is deliberately excluded. ``count`` is the total number of matching rows across all pages, distinct from the page length. Token-gated (staff tokens only).

schema.description: Exact ``event_type`` match, or the convenience value ``bounce`` (any of bounce_permanent / bounce_transient / bounce_other).
ISO-8601 datetime; inclusive lower bound on ``received_at``.
ISO-8601 datetime; inclusive upper bound on ``received_at``.

get.summary: List inbound SES events across all recipients

post.description: Accepts ``SubscriptionConfirmation``, ``UnsubscribeConfirmation``, and ``Notification`` SNS types. The inner SES payload uses either ``notificationType`` (identity notifications) or ``eventType`` (configuration-set destinations); both shapes are handled.

Message.description: Inner SES JSON payload as a string.

get.description: Returns the same signup analytics report used by Studio: normalized filters, headline cards, actionable source rows, pre-signup activity rows, and recent signup journey rows. Staff-token only, read-only. The response never includes raw IPs, raw user agents, full query strings, or anonymous IDs.

schema.description: Recent-signup row limit. Values above the safe cap are capped at 100.

get.summary: Read signup source and pre-signup activity analytics

get.description: Returns every sprint visible to the bearer token. Non-staff tokens see every sprint row -- visibility narrowing happens on the per-sprint detail endpoint, not on the collection.

schema.description: Filter to sprints with the given status.

post.description: Staff-only. Non-staff tokens get 403 with code ``forbidden_other_user_plan``. Slug uniqueness is enforced at the API layer (422) before hitting the DB.

event_series.description: Link to an event series, resolved by id (integer or numeric string) or slug (non-numeric string). ``null`` or an empty string clears the link; an unknown id/slug returns 422 ``unknown_series``.

422.description: Validation error (missing field, bad date, duplicate slug, or unknown ``event_series``).

delete.description: Sprint deletion is not available through the API (issue #864); operators must use Studio. DELETE returns a structured 405. Update sprint state with ``PATCH status=...`` instead.

example.error: Sprint deletion is not available through the API. Go to Studio to delete this sprint manually.

delete.summary: DELETE is not available on this route

get.description: Non-staff tokens see a sprint only if they have at least one plan in it; otherwise the endpoint returns 404 ``unknown_sprint`` (deliberately, to avoid leaking existence information).

404.description: Sprint not found or not visible.

event_series.description: Link to an event series, resolved by id (integer or numeric string) or slug (non-numeric string). ``null`` or an empty string clears the link; an unknown id/slug returns 422 ``unknown_series``.

422.description: Validation error (bad date, bad status, or unknown ``event_series``).

delete.description: Deletes both directed edges for a pair. Repeating the same removal is a successful no-op.

422.description: Missing field, unknown user, or self-pair.

delete.summary: Remove an accountability partner pair

get.description: Staff-token-only roster of enrolled sprint members and their current accountability partners. Non-enrolled users are never included.

post.description: Creates reciprocal manual partner edges for two enrolled members. Reposting an existing pair is idempotent and returns ``created: false``. If the pair was random, both directed rows are upgraded to ``source: manual`` and ``assigned_by`` becomes the bearer staff user.

200.description: Pair already existed or was promoted.

422.description: Missing field, unknown user, self-partnering, or non-enrolled participant.

post.description: Runs the same randomization rules as Studio: prior random assignments are cleared, manual assignments are preserved, unpartnered enrolled members are paired, and a final odd pool of three becomes a fully connected pod.

get.description: Staff sees every enrollment in the sprint; non-staff sees only their own row.

post.description: Tier mismatches are flagged as ``under_tier`` (warning) but the enrollment is still created -- staff are explicitly choosing to enroll the user.

delete.description: Sprint enrollment deletion is not available through the API (issue #864); unenroll a user in Studio instead. DELETE returns a structured 405.

example.error: Sprint enrollment deletion is not available through the API. Go to Studio to unenroll this user manually.

delete.summary: DELETE is not available on this route

post.description: Staff-token-only. Uses the same idempotent partner-intro email service as Studio. ``dry_run=true`` returns readiness counts, blockers, warnings, and recipient rows without sending email or writing partner-intro logs.

example.error: Body must be a JSON object

400.description: Invalid JSON body or non-object JSON body.

example.error: Partner intro email send is staff-only
Sprint is not ready for partner intro emails

422.description: Invalid dry_run value or sprint not ready.

get.description: Returns plans visible to the bearer (via ``visible_plans_for``), ordered by ``-created_at``.

post.description: Staff-only. Returns the nested plan detail shape.

send_ready_email.description: Optional. Defaults to false for compatibility. When true, staff plan creation sends the idempotent plan-ready transactional email.

example.error: Plan already exists for this user in this sprint

409.description: User already has a plan in this sprint.

422.description: Missing user_email, unknown user, bad goal length, bad title length, non-boolean send_ready_email, or unknown enum value.

post.description: Atomic: any single plan validation failure rolls every row back so the caller never has to clean up partial state. Returns the list of created plan ids.

409.description: One of the rows would create a duplicate plan.

post.description: Staff-token-only. Uses the same idempotent bulk service as Studio. ``dry_run=true`` returns the preview audience without sending email, creating notifications, creating EmailLog rows, stamping ``shared_at``, or writing ready-email logs.

example.error: Body must be a JSON object

400.description: Invalid JSON body or non-object JSON body.

get.description: Staff-token-only read API for next-sprint operations. The member set comes from the source sprint's enrollments. Evidence combines app-recorded source-plan done_at progress with CRM-held #plan-sprints Slack threads linked to the same source plan. The endpoint does not call Slack, enqueue ingest, parse messages, or mutate progress rows.

schema.description: Optional target sprint slug. When supplied, each member row includes target enrollment and target plan state for duplicate checks.

get.description: Staff-token-only read API for the same merged sprint roster activity data shown in Studio: enrolled rows first, plan-only rows after, checkpoint progress, latest local activity source, and current sprint-week triage state. Supports ``activity=no_update_this_week`` to return only rows needing follow-up during an active sprint week.

schema.description: Filter to no-plan or stale rows for the current active sprint week.

delete.description: Source deletion is intentionally unavailable through the API; use Studio.

example.error: Content sync source deletion is not available through the API. Go to Studio to delete this source manually.

delete.summary: DELETE is not available on this route

example.error: Content sync source deletion is not available through the API. Go to Studio to delete this source manually.

delete.summary: DELETE is not available on this route

post.description: Enqueues a sync job for the given source. Pass ``force=true`` as a query param or in the JSON body to bypass the per-source idempotency lock.

schema.description: Truthy values bypass the idempotency lock.

post.description: Grants the same long-lived ``TierOverride`` the Studio bulk contact-import gives non-paying members: a 10-year override (default tier ``main``) that deactivates any existing active override and records who granted it. A single grant is a one-element ``emails`` list. Missing users are created (``signup_source=imported``, ``email_verified=false``). Re-running an identical active grant is idempotent (``skipped_idempotent``, no new row). ``tier`` is validated against ``Tier`` -- unknown slug -> ``unknown_tier``; ``free`` / ``level==0`` -> ``invalid_tier``. Batch cap 1000.

400.description: Malformed body, unknown/invalid tier, or over-cap batch. Codes: ``missing_emails``, ``unknown_tier``, ``invalid_tier``, ``batch_too_large``.

get.description: Newest-first list of users with optional ``q`` search. Matches the Studio user-search surface: email, first/last name, ``stripe_customer_id``, ``slack_user_id``, and substring matches inside tags. Empty ``q`` returns the newest 50.

schema.description: Substring search across email/name/Stripe id/Slack id/tags.
ISO-8601 datetime; only users joined at or after this are returned.

post.description:
Consolidates the ``merge_email`` account into the ``canonical_email`` account: repoints every owned row, reconciles profile / entitlement fields by precedence (UNION tags, OR ``email_verified``, higher ``tier`` wins, surviving subscription dictates billing fields), records ``merge_email`` as an ``EmailAlias`` of canonical so future Stripe/relay events route correctly, and deactivates the secondary login. Runs in ONE transaction.

IRREVERSIBLE: there is no automated un-merge. Always run ``dry_run: true`` first to review the plan; a dry run computes the full plan against the real DB and persists NOTHING.

Errors: 400 ``self_merge`` (same normalized email); 404 ``user_not_found`` (unknown ``canonical_email``/``merge_email``, ``details.field`` says which); 409 ``subscription_conflict`` (both sides have a distinct live subscription -- refuses unless ``force: true``, never silently drops a paid sub); 409 ``staff_merge_refused`` (either side is staff -- requires ``force: true``); 422 ``unknown_field`` / ``validation_error`` (strict body).

example.error: Cannot merge an account into itself
Both accounts have a live subscription

409.description: Unresolved conflict: ``subscription_conflict`` (dual live subs) or ``staff_merge_refused``. Retry with ``force: true`` to proceed.

get.description: Staff-token queue of Stripe checkout identity conflicts. Filters are optional and can be combined. This endpoint is diagnostic only and never merges accounts.

patch.description: Non-destructive status update for a payment mismatch. Requires ``status`` of ``resolved`` or ``ignored`` and a non-empty ``resolution_note``. It never merges accounts.

422.description: Invalid status, unknown field, or empty note.

patch.summary: Mark payment mismatch resolved or ignored

patch.description: Accepts ``unsubscribed: bool`` and/or ``email_verified: true`` only. Setting ``email_verified: false`` returns 422 ``verification_demote_forbidden``. Any other field returns 422 ``unknown_field``. Every PATCH appends a ``CommunityAuditLog`` row (even no-ops).

email_verified.description: Only ``true`` is accepted; demote-via-API is forbidden.

get.description: Staff-only read endpoint for CRM/operator automation. Returns curated ``analytics.UserActivity`` rows using the same safe public/member-facing link policy as Studio CRM. No write endpoint exists for arbitrary activity rows.

schema.description: One of all, learning, events, content, account, comms. Default all.

post.description: Adds an ``EmailAlias`` routing ``alias_email`` -> the user identified by ``<email>``. Future Stripe webhooks whose billing email is the alias resolve to this canonical account instead of creating a duplicate. Idempotent: re-adding the same alias to the same owner returns 200, creates no duplicate row, and still writes an audit row. 409 ``alias_is_primary_email`` if the alias is already an existing primary ``User.email``; 409 ``alias_taken`` if it already routes to a DIFFERENT user; 422 on malformed email; 404 when ``<email>`` is unknown.

example.error: Alias email is already a primary account email

409.description: Alias collides with a primary email (``alias_is_primary_email``) or another user's alias (``alias_taken``).

example.error: alias_email is not a valid email

post.summary: Add an email alias (routes future Stripe events)

delete.description: Removes the ``EmailAlias`` routing ``<alias_email>`` to the user identified by ``<email>``. Idempotent: removing an alias that does not exist (or is not owned by this user) still returns 200 and writes an audit row. 404 when ``<email>`` is unknown.
delete.summary: Remove an email alias (idempotent)

post.description: Clears ``bounce_state`` back to ``none`` and resets ``bounce_recorded_at``, ``last_bounce_diagnostic``, and ``soft_bounce_count``. This endpoint deliberately does not resubscribe the user; ``unsubscribed`` remains unchanged.

reason.description: Optional operator note recorded in audit.

post.description: Staff-token repair/automation endpoint. Resolves the path email through primary email or email aliases, refuses users without submitted onboarding, then creates or reuses the single ``CRMRecord`` for the canonical user. This is meant for pre-existing onboarding submissions and account-merge repairs, not arbitrary CRM imports.

get.description: Newest-first list of ``EmailLog`` rows for the user. Each row carries the raw timing fields plus a derived ``disposition`` reflecting the strongest signal in the order ``sent < delivered < opened < clicked < bounced < complained``. ``kind`` filters on ``email_type`` exact match -- unknown values are valid (they simply return an empty page) since ``email_type`` is not a closed enum at the model layer.

Staff-only. Routed by three URL aliases under ``/api/users/<email>/`` -- the OpenAPI spec emits one operation per alias. ``?plan=null`` preserves the inbox-only behaviour; ``?plan=<id>`` narrows to one plan.

schema.description: Plan id, or the literal string ``null`` to narrow to notes with no plan attached.

422.description: Unknown user or bad plan filter.

post.description: Operator-triggered bounce mark for the rare cases where the SES -> SNS webhook is unavailable (e.g. SNS HTTPS subscription not yet wired) or where staff need to reproduce post-bounce state at will. Side-effects are shared with the webhook via ``accounts.utils.bounce`` so the resulting state is indistinguishable from a real bounce. Idempotent: calling again with the same ``bounce_type`` on a user already in that state returns 200, writes no new ``SesEvent`` row, but still records an audit row annotated ``no-op``.

example.reason: real bounce arrived; SNS not wired yet

diagnostic.description: Optional SMTP diagnostic to persist on ``User.last_bounce_diagnostic``.

reason.description: Optional free-form operator note recorded in the audit log.

200.description: User payload after the (possibly no-op) mark.

post.summary: Mark a user as bounced (mirrors the SES webhook)

get.description: Staff-only. Routed by three URL aliases under ``/api/users/<email>/`` -- the OpenAPI spec emits one operation per alias. ``?plan=null`` preserves the inbox-only behaviour; ``?plan=<id>`` narrows to one plan.

schema.description: Plan id, or the literal string ``null`` to narrow to notes with no plan attached.

422.description: Unknown user or bad plan filter.

get.description: Staff-only. Routed by three URL aliases under ``/api/users/<email>/`` -- the OpenAPI spec emits one operation per alias. ``?plan=null`` preserves the inbox-only behaviour; ``?plan=<id>`` narrows to one plan.

schema.description: Plan id, or the literal string ``null`` to narrow to notes with no plan attached.

422.description: Unknown user or bad plan filter.

get.description: Newest-first list of ``SesEvent`` rows for the user. Filters on ``SesEvent.user_id`` (NOT ``recipient_email``) so the history survives email renames. ``raw_payload`` is deliberately excluded -- the Studio surface owns the deep-dive.

post.description: Idempotent -- adding an existing tag is a no-op but still records an audit row. The input is normalized via ``accounts.utils.tags.normalize_tag``; empty input after normalization returns 422 ``invalid_tag``.

200.description: Current tag list including the new tag.

example.error: Tag must be a non-empty slug

delete.description: Idempotent -- removing a tag the user does not carry still returns 200 and writes an audit row. The ``<tag>`` path segment is re-normalized defensively.

200.description: Current tag list with the tag removed.

example.error: Tag must be a non-empty slug

delete.summary: Remove a single tag

get.description: Returns ``UtmCampaign`` rows ordered by ``-created_at``. Optional filters narrow the result.

post.description: Creates a campaign. ``slug`` must match ``^[a-z0-9_]+$`` and be unique. ``default_utm_source`` / ``default_utm_medium`` accept any value; presets (source: youtube, docs, github, linkedin, twitter, newsletter; medium: social, referral, email, cpc, display, organic, community) are guidance only.

422.description: Validation error (missing field, invalid or duplicate slug).

get.description: Returns the campaign plus a ``links`` array. By default only active links are included; pass ``?include_archived=true`` to also include archived links.

schema.description: Include archived links in the response.

get.summary: Retrieve a campaign with its links

patch.description: Partial update of ``name``, ``default_utm_source``, ``default_utm_medium``, ``notes``, and ``is_archived``. ``slug`` is locked once the campaign has links — attempting to change it on a campaign with links returns 422. Use ``is_archived=true`` to archive (no DELETE route).

422.description: Validation error (bad slug, duplicate slug, or slug change on a campaign with links).

post.description: Creates a link under the campaign. ``utm_content`` must match ``^[a-z0-9_]+$`` and be unique within the campaign. ``utm_source`` / ``utm_medium`` override the campaign defaults when non-empty and accept any value.

422.description: Validation error (missing field, invalid or duplicate utm_content).

get.description: Returns the link including the generated tracked ``url`` from ``build_url()`` plus ``effective_source`` / ``effective_medium``.

patch.description: Partial update of ``utm_content``, ``destination``, ``label``, ``utm_term``, ``utm_source``, ``utm_medium``, and ``is_archived``. Archive a link with ``is_archived=true`` (no DELETE route).

delete.summary: Delete a week

post.description: Creates a checkpoint at the optional ``position`` index inside the given week. Without ``position`` we append; with a ``position`` at a non-end value, existing siblings shift down by 1 inside a single ``transaction.atomic`` so concurrent inserts cannot leave a partial reorder.

404.description: Week not found or not visible.

delete.summary: Delete the singleton participant note

404.description: Week not found or not visible.

get.summary: Retrieve the singleton participant note for a week

patch.summary: Create or partially update the singleton participant note

put.summary: Create or replace the singleton participant note

get.description: Newest-first list of django-q tasks. Default returns the most recent 50 across success and failed. ``status``, ``group``, ``since``, and ``limit`` narrow the result.

schema.description: ISO-8601 datetime; only tasks started at or after this are returned.

get.description: Newest-first list of django-q tasks with ``success=False``. Use ``since`` to scope to a deploy.

schema.description: ISO-8601 datetime; only tasks started at or after this are returned.

get.description: Returns the full payload, timing, and result for one django-q ``Task`` row. Read-only — there is no retry/delete surface in the API.
